title: Secure Architecture Documentation
description: All security controls must be documented in the architecture and threat model before implementation.
mitigation: Include security requirements in architecture diagrams and design reviews.

title: Principle of Least Privilege
description: Components must operate with the minimum permissions required for their function.
mitigation: Enforce least privilege at every layer (network, OS, application).

title: Input Validation Strategy
description: All external input must be validated against a strict whitelist before processing.
mitigation: Implement centralized validation services.

title: Secure Credential Storage
description: Passwords must be hashed with a memory-hard function (Argon2id preferred).
mitigation: Use Argon2id with memory ≥ 64 MiB and appropriate iterations.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: MFA must be enforced for all privileged accounts and sensitive operations.
mitigation: Support TOTP, WebAuthn, or hardware tokens.

title: Credential Transmission
description: Credentials must be transmitted only over TLS 1.3 or higher.
mitigation: Enforce HSTS and reject weak TLS versions.

title: Enforce Authorization Checks
description: Every API endpoint and UI action must perform explicit authorization checks.
mitigation: Use centralized authorization middleware.

title: Role-Based Access
description: Role-based access control must follow the principle of least privilege.
mitigation: Define roles centrally and review periodically.

title: Server-Side Input Validation
description: All input must be validated on the server using a strict whitelist.
mitigation: Use schema-based validators (Pydantic, JSON Schema).

title: Output Encoding
description: All output must be contextually encoded to prevent XSS.
mitigation: Use DOMPurify (JS) or OWASP Java Encoder.

title: Strong Cryptographic Primitives
description: Use only approved algorithms and key lengths (AES-256, SHA-256, etc.).
mitigation: Avoid custom crypto; use established libraries.

title: Secure Randomness
description: All random values must be generated with a cryptographically secure PRNG.
mitigation: Use secrets module or os.urandom.

title: Secure Logging
description: Logs must not contain sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII).
mitigation: Sanitize logs before writing.

title: Generic Error Messages
description: Error messages must not disclose internal implementation details.
mitigation: Return generic messages to users.

title: Secure Session Management
description: Session tokens must be generated securely and invalidated on logout.
mitigation: Use HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict flags.

title: Prevent SQL Injection
description: Use parameterized queries or ORM prepared statements for all database access.
mitigation: Never concatenate user input into SQL.

title: Prevent Command Injection
description: Avoid passing untrusted data to OS commands or shell functions.
mitigation: Use safe APIs or whitelisted commands.

title: Prevent Path Traversal
description: Normalize and validate all file paths against a known safe directory.
mitigation: Use pathlib and canonical path checks.

title: Secure File Upload
description: Restrict uploaded files to allowed types and scan for malware.
mitigation: Validate MIME type and content; store outside web root.

title: Prevent Reflected XSS
description: Escape all untrusted data before rendering in HTML/JS contexts.
mitigation: Use templating engines with auto-escaping.

title: Account Lockout Protection
description: Implement progressive delay or CAPTCHA after failed login attempts.
mitigation: Avoid permanent lockouts; use temporary delays.

title: Key Management
description: Cryptographic keys must be stored securely and rotated periodically.
mitigation: Use hardware security modules or secret managers.

title: API Security Controls
description: All APIs must enforce authentication, authorization, and rate limiting.
mitigation: Use OAuth2/JWT with short expiry and scopes.

title: Prevent Insecure Deserialization
description: Never deserialize untrusted data without strict validation.
mitigation: Use safe deserializers or avoid deserialization.

title: Secure Configuration Management
description: Sensitive configuration must never be stored in code or version control.
mitigation: Use environment variables or secret managers.
